summary:
The limitations of fine needle aspiration include insufficient accuracy for follicular thyroid cancer due to similar cell patterns as benign tumors; also inadequate specificity for some types of breast and lung cancers without additional confirmatory tests or biopsies are needed.
To improve precision especially when dealing with indeterminate results, further histopathological examination through surgical excisional biopsy should often follow initial FNAC findings. Agent1 reasoned that while FNAC is effective for many diagnoses including thyroid papillary carcinoma and general use in lungs, its sensitivity drops notably for follicular thyroid carcinoma and select scenarios within breasts and lungs requiring more robust confirmation methods such as surgery-based tissue analysis post-FNAC evaluation if necessary. This aligns closely with identifying situations needing stronger evidence beyond what a simple aspirate offers clinically speaking regarding these malignancies' diagnosis certainty levels based solely formatted_summary:
agent1 (Round 1 summarized): The limitations of fine needle aspiration include insufficient accuracy for follicular thyroid cancer due to similar cell patterns as benign tumors; also inadequate specificity for some types of breast and lung cancers without additional confirmatory tests or biopsies are needed.
To improve precision especially when dealing with indeterminate results, further histopathological examination through surgical excisional biopsy should often follow initial FNAC findings. Agent1 reasoned that while FNAC is effective for many diagnoses including thyroid papillary carcinoma and general use in lungs, its sensitivity drops notably for follicular thyroid carcinoma and select scenarios within breasts and lungs requiring more robust confirmation methods such as surgery-based tissue analysis post-FNAC evaluation if necessary. This aligns closely with identifying situations needing stronger evidence beyond what a simple aspirate offers clinically speaking regarding these malignancies' diagnosis certainty levels based solely
